chunks.text: My grandfather worked for many years as the keeper of a lighthouse in a seaside town.
That town was the place where he lived his whole life.
Every night he would climb the stairs of the tower.
He checked the lamp, wiped the windows, and when a storm approached
he worked through the whole night without sleep.
Every summer I visited his house and looked up at the light.
The moment when the lighthouse began to glow was the time when I most wished to be there.
Once he revealed to me the reason why he had continued this work.
" So that ships that come back from across the sea can enter the harbor safe," he said.
I often think of the way he guided sailors.
Between my grandfather and the lighthouse was a long correspond that needed no words.
How many sailors his light had saved on stormy nights, I could never imagine.
